evidence.observation: The 16 px sword remains readable through a continuous diagonal blade, compact guard, separated grip, and a limited value ramp.
The matching armor reuses material hues while reserving its broadest dark values for the outer silhouette and plate separation.

observations.statement: At inventory scale, continuous tool contours and separated functional parts carry more identity than isolated decorative pixels.
A shared palette can identify one material across weapon and armor shapes without forcing those shapes to match.
Highlights follow blade or plate direction, while accent pixels stay sparse enough to preserve the base material.

derivedRules.statement: Validate that the outer contour and functional zones identify the equipment archetype at native inventory scale.
Treat palette and silhouette as independent controls so one material can support a coherent equipment family.
Confine accent colors to guards, edges, fasteners, or motifs instead of replacing the dominant material ramp.
